title: TOTP
description: TOTP is a field-specialized multi-input verification component for one-time codes. It manages digit-level focus, paste handling, keyboard navigation, and configurable code length while following the shared field state model.
intro: Use `ui-totp` for two-factor authentication, device verification, SMS codes, and short PIN-style confirmation flows. It inherits the shared field API for labels, help text, size, variant, readonly, disabled, and validation messaging while adding grouped digit inputs and verification-specific interaction behavior.

featureSections.title: Basic verification input
featureSections.description: The default 6-digit setup covers the common authenticator and SMS verification case, with paste support and digit-by-digit entry built in.

featureSections.title: Digit count and scale
featureSections.description: Shorter and longer code lengths fit different verification contexts. Size and digit count should be chosen together so the code stays easy to scan and enter.

featureSections.title: Appearance and size
featureSections.description: TOTP inputs inherit the shared field visual treatments, so they can match compact auth dialogs or roomier account-verification surfaces.

featureSections.title: Readonly, disabled, and error states
featureSections.description: Readonly is useful for displaying generated or restored codes, disabled removes interaction entirely, and inline error text should communicate failed verification clearly.

featureSections.title: Validation with reactive forms
featureSections.description: When a full code is required before continuing the flow, use reactive-form validation so incomplete input behaves consistently with the rest of the form.

featureSections.title: Verification panel composition
featureSections.description: A stronger end-to-end example: verification context, grouped code input, and the next available actions inside a focused auth surface.

accessibility.title: Grouped input semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-totp` exposes the digit inputs inside a container with `role="group"`. The component uses either the visible label or a fallback group label so assistive technologies announce the code entry context once and then each digit position individually.

accessibility.title: Keyboard and paste behavior
accessibility.body:
| Key / action | Behavior |
| --- | --- |
| Typing a digit | Fills the current input and moves focus to the next digit. |
| Backspace | Clears the current digit or moves back and clears the previous one when current is empty. |
| Arrow Left / Right | Moves between digits according to text direction. |
| Paste | Splits pasted numeric content across the remaining digit inputs. |
| Home / End | Not used; standard field navigation is digit-to-digit instead. |

accessibility.title: Labels and descriptions
accessibility.body: Each digit input gets its own accessible label such as `Digit 1 of 6`, while help and error text are linked through `aria-describedby`. This keeps the grouped code understandable without forcing the user to infer position from visual layout alone.

apiSections.title: TotpComponent - ui-totp
apiSections.lead: Standalone grouped verification-code component built on the shared field base. It manages digit-level state and navigation while exposing a single combined string value to forms and consumers.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Number of digit inputs to render.
Optional accessible group label when no visible label is present.
Marks the field as required in surrounding forms.
Keeps the code visible but blocks editing.
Visual treatment applied to each digit wrapper.
Optional digit placeholder source; single-char placeholders repeat, matching-length strings distribute per digit.

blocks.title: Value shape and behavior
rows: Combined code value exposed through CVA and change output.
Emits the current combined code whenever a digit changes.
Splits an incoming string value across the rendered digit inputs.

paragraphs: The component extends the shared `FieldComponent`, so focus and validation messaging integrate with the rest of the field family even though the rendered UI is a group of inputs.

Use `digitsCount` to match the backend verification contract. The component does not validate whether the entered code is correct; it only handles grouped input behavior and exposes the combined string.
